articles.title: GIVE YOUR CREATIVITY ROOM TO BREATHE 🧘
TRY ASANA FOR FREE — NO CREDIT CARD REQUIRED
AN UNRELIABLE FUTURE
articles.summary: Digital infrastructure is becoming increasingly fragile because a few cloud providers dominate the market. AWS, Azure, and GCP control over 60% of global cloud capacity. Modern systems are highly complex, with billions of lines of code, microservices, and AI workloads that stress data centers. Small errors or capacity bottlenecks can trigger cascading outages, as seen in recent disruptions. Physical risks and high occupancy levels compound the problem. To reduce vulnerability, governments and enterprises are adopting multi-cloud strategies and on-premise repatriation to ensure that single failures do not paralyze operations.

articles.title: BRAND MARKETERS CAN'T SEE WHERE THEIR INFLUENCER BUDGETS GO
articles.summary: Many brand marketers lack visibility into influencer budgets, with 61% unsure of agency payments to creators. 55% plan to change their approach within a year due to opaque fees and limited benchmarks. In-house management is growing, currently at 16%, offering direct influencer relationships, faster content production, and better budget efficiency.

articles.title: OUTCOME-BASED POSITIONING
articles.summary: SaaS pricing is shifting from selling tools to selling completed work. Constant Contact shows an iterative path by rewriting plan copy around actions such as “do it yourself”, “do it with us”, and “done for you”. Lindy takes a radical path by adding a fake Human Assistant plan that compares its AI Work Assistant to human labor. This approach makes the value of outcomes more obvious and may spread across agentic AI products.

articles.title: HOW TO PRICE YOUR PRODUCT: A PRICING MODEL DECISION TREE
articles.summary: This guide presents a pricing model decision tree to align how customers pay with how they experience value. Start with buyer type and product value, then narrow into seat, usage, outcome, or platform models. Seat pricing works when value grows per user, usage pricing fits when consumption maps to benefit, outcome pricing ties revenue to measurable results, and platform pricing supports multi team adoption. Most companies use hybrid models.

articles.title: HOW MARKETERS ARE CREATING HIGH QUALITY CONTENT WITH AI THAT'S NOT SLOP (WEBINAR)
articles.summary: This webinar, on February 24, will show how to use AI to write content without becoming generic. It will cover which inputs produce valuable output, how to turn research and expert knowledge into standout content, and provide a repeatable framework for using AI as leverage, not a crutch.

articles.title: IT'S TIME TO HIRE YOUR FIRST MARKETING AGENT
articles.summary: AI agents are outcome-focused tools that act across platforms to automate tasks, unlike chatbots or rule-based workflows that require constant direction. Agents start with a clearly defined role, a specific task, and are built and refined using real outputs. In marketing, agents can handle competitive intelligence, content repurposing, or growth analytics. For example, a competitive intelligence agent scans LinkedIn, YouTube, and G2 to generate weekly reports on competitors. A content repurposing agent turns webinar transcripts into draft social posts while referencing brand guides and past content. Tools like Relay.app and Mutiny enable automating and delivering these agents' outputs.

articles.title: HOW TO USE THE MAGIC (AND SCIENCE) OF RITUALS TO IMPROVE YOUR WRITING
articles.summary: Rituals, unlike simple routines, carry symbolic meaning that helps shift mindset and improve performance. Research shows they can reduce stress and prime focus even without religious belief. Writers like Stephen King use consistent pre-writing rituals to signal to the brain that it is time to create. Experts say that repeating specific actions in the same place builds mental associations that make it easier to enter a productive state. Whether religious or secular, rituals work because action shapes psychology and reinforces identity.

articles.title: THE FUTURE OF BRAND CREATIVITY BELONGS TO THE SMALL AND RECKLESS
articles.summary: Large holding companies are adopting AI out of fear of being left behind rather than a clear creative strategy. By standardizing AI, they are reinforcing systems built to reduce variance, which leads to faster production of safe, undifferentiated work. As advanced AI tools are widely accessible, scale is no longer a durable moat, and bureaucracy becomes a drag on originality. At the same time, experienced talent is leaving large networks, lowering barriers for smaller agencies to compete.

articles.title: CHATGPT ADS SPOTTED, AND THEY ARE QUITE AGGRESSIVE
articles.summary: ChatGPT ads have been spotted by US users, and they're appearing on the very first prompt.

articles.title: DIGITAL PR IN THE US VS UK: MAPPING THE DIFFERENCES
articles.summary: The UK focuses on press releases, while the US favors newswires and skips full releases in emails.
